explanations.2: This loads a shell script file into the current test so we can use the functions defined in it.
explanations.3: This creates an array (a list) of strings that will be passed as arguments to simulate how a user would run the command.
explanations.4: The 'run' keyword executes the function and captures what it prints and what exit code it returns, so we can check those things in the next line.
explanations.5: We're checking that the output matches a pattern (a regex) instead of being an exact string, so it works even if the filename in the error message changes.
